articles.title: Congress passes the largest housing affordability bill in decades
articles.summary: An expansive bipartisan bill intended to bring down the cost of housing by boosting the supply of homes has passed both houses of Congress and is headed to the president's desk for a signature.
articles.titleZh: 国会通过数十年来最大的住房负担能力法案
articles.summaryZh: 旨在通过增加住房供应来降低住房成本的一项广泛的两党法案已在国会参众两院获得通过，并将送交总统签署。

contentParagraphs.en: Workers build the roofing structure of a new home under construction on July 15, 2025, in Richardson, Texas.
contentParagraphs.zh: Workers build the roofing structure of a new home under construction on July 15, 2025, in Richardson, Texas.

contentParagraphs.en: If there's room for agreement on anything in Washington, it's that lawmakers need to do something to make homeownership more affordable. On Tuesday, legislators on both sides of the aisle clinched the final vote in the House to pass the largest piece of housing legislation in decades.
contentParagraphs.zh: If there's room for agreement on anything in Washington, it's that lawmakers need to do something to make homeownership more affordable. On Tuesday, legislators on both sides of the aisle clinched the final vote in the House to pass the largest piece of housing legislation in decades.

contentParagraphs.en: The bill, called the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, passed 358-32 in the House. The Senate approved it Monday with similarly overwhelming bipartisan support. It now heads to President Trump's desk for his signature.
contentParagraphs.zh: The bill, called the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, passed 358-32 in the House. The Senate approved it Monday with similarly overwhelming bipartisan support. It now heads to President Trump's desk for his signature.

contentParagraphs.en: In an interview with NPR, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., a co-sponsor of the bill, said housing affordability has become a priority for Congress.
contentParagraphs.zh: In an interview with NPR, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., a co-sponsor of the bill, said housing affordability has become a priority for Congress.

contentParagraphs.en: "Every time every member of Congress goes back home they hear how urgent it is to bring down home prices. And that's what the bill does," she said.
contentParagraphs.zh: "Every time every member of Congress goes back home they hear how urgent it is to bring down home prices. And that's what the bill does," she said.

contentParagraphs.en: A number of factors have made homes out of reach for many U.S. buyers. According to the real estate broker Redfin, a family needs an income of about $117,000 a year to afford the typical home on the market, almost $30,000 more than what most U.S. households earn.
contentParagraphs.zh: A number of factors have made homes out of reach for many U.S. buyers. According to the real estate broker Redfin, a family needs an income of about $117,000 a year to afford the typical home on the market, almost $30,000 more than what most U.S. households earn.

contentParagraphs.en: Mortgage rates have also risen over the past several years, and that boosts the monthly cost of ownership. Rates had been falling earlier this year, but the war in Iran raised the cost of borrowing, and the nationwide average is now about 6.5%. Families also have less purchasing power, as inflation has outstripped wages.
contentParagraphs.zh: Mortgage rates have also risen over the past several years, and that boosts the monthly cost of ownership. Rates had been falling earlier this year, but the war in Iran raised the cost of borrowing, and the nationwide average is now about 6.5%. Families also have less purchasing power, as inflation has outstripped wages.

contentParagraphs.en: But the main issue this bill tries to address is that the U.S. does not have enough houses to keep up with demand. Realtor.com estimated that last year the U.S. was short by more than 4 million housing units.
contentParagraphs.zh: But the main issue this bill tries to address is that the U.S. does not have enough houses to keep up with demand. Realtor.com estimated that last year the U.S. was short by more than 4 million housing units.

contentParagraphs.en: "Supply is the key problem here," said Jeanna Kenney, assistant professor of economics, finance and real estate at Villanova University. "Anything you can do to make supply easier is going to be helpful in the long term."
contentParagraphs.zh: "Supply is the key problem here," said Jeanna Kenney, assistant professor of economics, finance and real estate at Villanova University. "Anything you can do to make supply easier is going to be helpful in the long term."

contentParagraphs.en: Ban on corporate investors that buy hundreds of homes
contentParagraphs.zh: Ban on corporate investors that buy hundreds of homes

contentParagraphs.en: Rather than making a single change, the bill is a hodgepodge of provisions designed to either encourage housing construction or make it easier for home seekers to buy. The flashiest part of the package is a ban that prevents corporate investors from buying up more single-family homes to rent out. If one of those groups already owns at least 350 houses, it won't be able to buy others.
contentParagraphs.zh: Rather than making a single change, the bill is a hodgepodge of provisions designed to either encourage housing construction or make it easier for home seekers to buy. The flashiest part of the package is a ban that prevents corporate investors from buying up more single-family homes to rent out. If one of those groups already owns at least 350 houses, it won't be able to buy others.

contentParagraphs.en: This provision was one of the most contested as the bill worked its way through the legislative process. Some politicians endorsed it as a move to stop corporate landlords from being able to outbid families and buy up large chunks of local housing markets with cash offers.
contentParagraphs.zh: This provision was one of the most contested as the bill worked its way through the legislative process. Some politicians endorsed it as a move to stop corporate landlords from being able to outbid families and buy up large chunks of local housing markets with cash offers.

contentParagraphs.en: But nationally, these investors make up only about 3% of the single-family rental market. And some experts warn the ban could actually limit the supply of available homes, because investors often buy and fix up homes that would otherwise fall out of the market. "It chills investment, and we need more investment in housing stock, not less," said Ross Marchand, executive director of the right-leaning think tank the Taxpayers Protection Alliance.
contentParagraphs.zh: But nationally, these investors make up only about 3% of the single-family rental market. And some experts warn the ban could actually limit the supply of available homes, because investors often buy and fix up homes that would otherwise fall out of the market. "It chills investment, and we need more investment in housing stock, not less," said Ross Marchand, executive director of the right-leaning think tank the Taxpayers Protection Alliance.

contentParagraphs.en: Still, Warren hailed the investor ban, pointing out that investors are buying up a large slice of the market in some parts of the country, such as Atlanta. "If you don't live in a neighborhood where private equity has already moved in, believe me, you're on their list," Warren said.
contentParagraphs.zh: Still, Warren hailed the investor ban, pointing out that investors are buying up a large slice of the market in some parts of the country, such as Atlanta. "If you don't live in a neighborhood where private equity has already moved in, believe me, you're on their list," Warren said.

contentParagraphs.en: Streamlining regulations for homebuilders
contentParagraphs.zh: Streamlining regulations for homebuilders

contentParagraphs.en: While the legislation doesn't provide new federal dollars for homebuilding, it streamlines some of the regulations homebuilders must follow to get existing federal financing.
contentParagraphs.zh: While the legislation doesn't provide new federal dollars for homebuilding, it streamlines some of the regulations homebuilders must follow to get existing federal financing.

contentParagraphs.en: For example, it allows builders to skip the environmental review when a housing project is going up between two buildings that have already gone through the process.
contentParagraphs.zh: For example, it allows builders to skip the environmental review when a housing project is going up between two buildings that have already gone through the process.

contentParagraphs.en: A different provision creates a grant program for communities to develop "pattern books" of preapproved housing designs, so builders won't need as many approvals to get up to code.
contentParagraphs.zh: A different provision creates a grant program for communities to develop "pattern books" of preapproved housing designs, so builders won't need as many approvals to get up to code.

contentParagraphs.en: Another is aimed at making manufactured homes more affordable by getting rid of the rule that those houses must have a permanent chassis, or a steel frame that makes them movable. Manufactured homes are often installed onto permanent foundations, and housing policy experts say that removing the chassis requirement could cut $5,000 to $10,000 off construction costs and allow for designs that could more easily incorporate a second story or basement.
contentParagraphs.zh: Another is aimed at making manufactured homes more affordable by getting rid of the rule that those houses must have a permanent chassis, or a steel frame that makes them movable. Manufactured homes are often installed onto permanent foundations, and housing policy experts say that removing the chassis requirement could cut $5,000 to $10,000 off construction costs and allow for designs that could more easily incorporate a second story or basement.

contentParagraphs.en: "Not having that chassis immediately wipes several thousand dollars off that price — and this is already a type of home that is significantly less expensive than a traditional stick-built home," said Kate Wood, a lending expert at the financial advice website NerdWallet. (Stick-built is a real estate term for wooden homes that are constructed on site, rather than prefabricated.)
contentParagraphs.zh: "Not having that chassis immediately wipes several thousand dollars off that price — and this is already a type of home that is significantly less expensive than a traditional stick-built home," said Kate Wood, a lending expert at the financial advice website NerdWallet. (Stick-built is a real estate term for wooden homes that are constructed on site, rather than prefabricated.)

contentParagraphs.en: The bill also encourages local governments to speed up the homebuilding process by giving more federal dollars to places that build more housing. "If you don't build more housing, you should lose those incentives. And they should go to the places where you're building more housing," said the bill's co-sponsor, Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., on the Senate floor Monday ahead of the chamber's vote.
contentParagraphs.zh: The bill also encourages local governments to speed up the homebuilding process by giving more federal dollars to places that build more housing. "If you don't build more housing, you should lose those incentives. And they should go to the places where you're building more housing," said the bill's co-sponsor, Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., on the Senate floor Monday ahead of the chamber's vote.

contentParagraphs.en: What federal legislation can't do
contentParagraphs.zh: What federal legislation can't do

contentParagraphs.en: Washington lawmakers have a limited role when it comes to homebuilding — local governments have a much bigger say over construction. So do private builders, who are facing external challenges like the high cost of labor and supplies.
contentParagraphs.zh: Washington lawmakers have a limited role when it comes to homebuilding — local governments have a much bigger say over construction. So do private builders, who are facing external challenges like the high cost of labor and supplies.

contentParagraphs.en: And Congress doesn't get a vote on mortgage rates. With rising inflation, the Fed might actually raise interest rates later this year.
contentParagraphs.zh: And Congress doesn't get a vote on mortgage rates. With rising inflation, the Fed might actually raise interest rates later this year.

contentParagraphs.en: But this is still the largest housing affordability bill to come out of Congress in decades, and researchers and those in the housing industry say it could help make homeownership more accessible.
contentParagraphs.zh: But this is still the largest housing affordability bill to come out of Congress in decades, and researchers and those in the housing industry say it could help make homeownership more accessible.

articles.title: In the Ebola epicenter, a gold-mining town reacts with fear, disbelief and grit
articles.summary: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.
articles.titleZh: 在埃博拉疫情中心，一座金矿小镇的反应充满了恐惧、怀疑和勇气
articles.summaryZh: NPR 来自刚果民主共和国蒙布瓦卢的报道。遏制病毒的斗争面临着供应不足以及居民怀疑病毒真实性等障碍。

contentParagraphs.en: Women sift gold-bearing sediment in search of gold at the Maidede mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo's Ituri province on June 16.
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 16 日，妇女们在刚果民主共和国伊图里省迈德德矿筛分含金沉积物寻找黄金。

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: MONGBWALU, Democratic Republic of Congo — Joseph Mute witnessed a string of mysterious deaths in Mongbwalu long before the Congolese government declared an Ebola outbreak.
contentParagraphs.zh: 刚果民主共和国蒙布瓦卢——早在刚果政府宣布埃博拉疫情爆发之前，约瑟夫·穆特就在蒙布瓦卢目睹了一系列神秘死亡事件。

contentParagraphs.en: A neighborhood leader in the town, Mute said that the characteristic feature of these deaths was the presence of blood.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该镇的一位社区领袖穆特说，这些死亡事件的一个特点就是身上有血迹。

contentParagraphs.en: "They had blood in the nose, blood in the mouth," he said, standing on an unpaved road in the Shuni neighborhood.
contentParagraphs.zh: “他们鼻子里有血，嘴里有血，”他站在舒尼社区一条未铺砌的道路上说。

contentParagraphs.en: A gold-mining town of about 130,000 people located in Ituri province, Mongbwalu is one of the epicenters of eastern Congo's Ebola outbreak. The outbreak is believed to have started here, according to the World Health Organization, but this has yet to be fully confirmed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 蒙布瓦卢是伊图里省的一个金矿小镇，约有 13 万人口，是刚果东部埃博拉疫情的震中之一。据世界卫生组织称，疫情据信是从这里开始的，但这一点尚未得到充分证实。

contentParagraphs.en: From Mongbwalu, Ebola likely spread across Ituri, including to its capital, Bunia, a city of more than 1 million. Confirmed cases have also appeared in the Congolese provinces of North Kivu and South Kivu, as well as in Uganda, which shares a long border with Ituri.
contentParagraphs.zh: 埃博拉病毒可能从蒙布瓦卢传播到整个伊图里，包括其首都布尼亚，这座人口超过 100 万的城市。刚果的北基伍省和南基伍省以及与伊图里有漫长边界的乌干达也出现了确诊病例。

contentParagraphs.en: A cemetery in Mongbwalu, in Ituri province, in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. The gold-mining town is one of the hot spots of Congo's Ebola outbreak. Frequent burials have become part of daily life as the virus continues to spread and take lives.
contentParagraphs.zh: 刚果民主共和国东部伊图里省蒙布瓦卢的一座公墓。这个金矿小镇是刚果埃博拉疫情的热点地区之一。随着病毒继续传播并夺走生命，频繁的埋葬已成为日常生活的一部分。

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: At first, it wasn't clear what was causing the deaths. Mongbwalu's population is made up largely of poor gold panners, and diseases are common among this marginalized group. Some, Mute said, suggested that the spreading sickness was tuberculosis, AIDS or even mercury poisoning — from the chemical that miners use to extract gold from ore.
contentParagraphs.zh: 起初，尚不清楚造成死亡的原因。蒙布瓦鲁的人口主要由贫穷的淘金者组成，疾病在这个边缘群体中很常见。穆特说，一些人认为正在传播的疾病是肺结核、艾滋病，甚至是汞中毒——来自矿工从矿石中提取黄金时使用的化学物质。

contentParagraphs.en: Others offered a supernatural explanation, which gained wide belief.
contentParagraphs.zh: 其他人则提出了超自然的解释，得到了广泛的相信。

contentParagraphs.en: A woman carrying a funeral cross rides on a motorcycle taxi through Bunia, the capital of Ituri province, in the Democratic Republic of Congo, on June 18. Funeral processions and other signs of mourning have become increasingly common as eastern Congo battles an Ebola outbreak.
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 18 日，一名背着葬礼十字架的妇女乘坐摩托车穿过刚果民主共和国伊图里省首府布尼亚。随着刚果东部抗击埃博拉疫情，送葬队伍和其他哀悼活动变得越来越普遍。

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: "Flames of the coffin"
contentParagraphs.zh: 《棺材里的火焰》

contentParagraphs.en: In February, mourners traveled from Bunia to Mongbwalu in a car, carrying a coffin that was damaged en route.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2月，哀悼者乘坐汽车从布尼亚前往蒙布瓦卢，他们载着一口在途中受损的棺材。

contentParagraphs.en: Family members of the deceased decided to get a new coffin when they got to Mongbwalu. They burned the old one in the Shuni neighborhood, violating a traditional taboo. Soon after, residents of Shuni began to sicken and die, giving rise to rumors of a cursed coffin.
contentParagraphs.zh: 死者家属到达蒙布瓦鲁后决定购买新棺材。他们在舒尼社区烧毁了旧的，违反了传统禁忌。不久之后，舒尼的居民开始生病和死亡，并引发了关于被诅咒的棺材的谣言。

contentParagraphs.en: "People said it was the flames of the coffin spreading through the neighborhood," said Mute, referring to the then-unidentified disease. "But that's not true."
contentParagraphs.zh: 穆特说：“人们说是棺材的火焰在附近蔓延。”他指的是当时尚未确诊的疾病。 “但事实并非如此。”

contentParagraphs.en: Congo's government eventually identified the illness as Ebola, but only after a significant delay.
contentParagraphs.zh: 刚果政府最终将这种疾病确定为埃博拉病毒，但只是在严重拖延之后。

contentParagraphs.en: A view of the morgue at the Ebola treatment center of the Evangelical Medical Center in Bunia on June 18, as staff prepare the body of a person who died from Ebola. The facility is part of the region's response to the outbreak spreading through eastern Congo.
contentParagraphs.zh: 6 月 18 日，布尼亚福音派医疗中心埃博拉治疗中心停尸房内的景象，工作人员正在准备一名埃博拉死者的尸体。该设施是该地区应对刚果东部疫情蔓延的措施的一部分。

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: The first-known suspected case was of a nurse who developed fever and vomiting on April 24, according to the country's health ministry. The nurse died in Bunia but was buried in Mongbwalu.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据该国卫生部称，第一例已知疑似病例是一名护士，她于 4 月 24 日出现发烧和呕吐症状。这名护士在布尼亚去世，但被埋葬在蒙布瓦卢。

contentParagraphs.en: Both fever and vomiting are hallmark symptoms of Ebola. Bleeding, though less common, is also a symptom of severe infection.
contentParagraphs.zh: 发烧和呕吐都是埃博拉病毒的标志症状。出血虽然不太常见，但也是严重感染的症状。

contentParagraphs.en: In early May, four health workers in Mongbwalu died within four days, sparking more alarm. But testing for Ebola at Congo's National Institute for Biomedical Research initially came up negative because scientists screened for the Zaire and Sudan species of the virus.
contentParagraphs.zh: 5月初，Mongbwalu的四名卫生工作者在四天内死亡，引发了更多恐慌。但刚果国家生物医学研究所的埃博拉病毒检测最初结果呈阴性，因为科学家对扎伊尔和苏丹的埃博拉病毒进行了筛查。

contentParagraphs.en: On May 15, the government at last declared an outbreak, after genomic sequencing confirmed that the rarer Bundibugyo species of the virus was circulating. By that point, more than 50 people had already died in the Shuni neighborhood, Mute said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 5 月 15 日，在基因组测序证实更罕见的 Bundibugyo 病毒正在传播后，政府最终宣布爆发疫情。穆特说，当时 Shuni 社区已经有 50 多人死亡。

contentParagraphs.en: "It hurts me," he said, pointing out houses in the neighborhood that now lie empty because the former occupants had died or fled.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这让我很伤心，”他指着附近的房屋说道，这些房屋现在因为以前的居住者已经死亡或逃跑而空置。

contentParagraphs.en: Serge Ugena of Mongbwalu lost his wife and five family members to Ebola as the outbreak swept through the Shuni neighborhood, one of the hardest-hit areas of the town.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当埃博拉疫情席卷该镇受灾最严重的地区之一舒尼（Shuni）社区时，蒙布瓦卢（Mongbwalu）的塞尔吉·乌格纳（Serge Ugena）失去了妻子和五名家人。

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: Gold mining is a factor in the spread
contentParagraphs.zh: 金矿开采是价差的一个因素

contentParagraphs.en: Ebola infections are still spreading widely in Mongbwalu over one month after the outbreak was declared. The town and surrounding area have 220 out of the 1,003 confirmed cases in the country as of June 20, according to the health ministry.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在宣布疫情爆发一个多月后，埃博拉感染仍在蒙布瓦卢广泛传播。据卫生部称，截至 6 月 20 日，该镇及周边地区已有 1,003 例确诊病例，其中 220 例为该镇及周边地区。

contentParagraphs.en: However, aid workers believe that the official numbers vastly understate the scale of the crisis. Many people avoid seeking medical care if they get ill. Superstition and fear run deep. Testing delays have also meant that some probable Ebola victims died without being confirmed as having the disease.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而，援助人员认为，官方数字大大低估了危机的规模。许多人在生病时会避免就医。迷信和恐惧根深蒂固。检测的延误还意味着一些可能的埃博拉受害者在没有被确认患有这种疾病的情况下就死亡了。

contentParagraphs.en: A view of the Shuni neighborhood in Mongbwalu. The neighborhood is one of the epicenters of the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo. Death tolls here are among the town's highest.
contentParagraphs.zh: Mongbwalu Shuni 社区的景色。该社区是刚果东部埃博拉疫情的震中之一。这里的死亡人数是该镇最高的。

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: Arsène Mpiana for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Arsène Mpiana

contentParagraphs.en: In some parts of Mongbwalu, locals have fiercely resisted health responders too. Rumors are rife: Some believe that aid groups are spreading the disease to enrich themselves.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在蒙布瓦鲁的一些地区，当地人也强烈抵制卫生救援人员。谣言甚嚣尘上：一些人认为援助组织传播疾病是为了让自己中饱私囊。

contentParagraphs.en: Funerals are also a flash point. This month, police in the town fired warning shots and tear gas to try to disperse a crowd that had come to seize a coffin of a suspected Ebola victim. Many families still wish to bury their dead themselves, despite the risks.
contentParagraphs.zh: 葬礼也是一个热点。本月，该镇警察鸣枪示警并使用催泪瓦斯，试图驱散前来抢夺疑似埃博拉受害者棺材的人群。尽管存在风险，许多家庭仍然希望自己埋葬死者。

contentParagraphs.en: The rapid spread of Ebola over a large range reflects the nature of gold mining, the bedrock of Mongbwalu's economy. Muddy pits filled with miners surround the town. They work closely, digging, pumping water through sluices and sifting for gold ore. The mine
contentParagraphs.zh: 埃博拉病毒在大范围内的迅速传播反映了金矿开采的性质，金矿开采是蒙布瓦鲁经济的基石。小镇周围满是泥泞的坑，里面挤满了矿工。他们密切合作，挖掘、通过水闸抽水并筛选金矿石。矿山
